light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Fair to good trout fishing conditions today on the Toccoa River with recent stocking enhancing catch opportunities. Water temperature is unavailable, but flow and gage height remain stable.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is the morning of day 1 due to moderate sky cover and minimal rain, providing comfortable feeding conditions and balanced nymph fishing opportunities.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current flow is 230 cfs with a gage height of 1.21 ft. Water temperature and turbidity data are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Partly cloudy skies with 33% average sky cover, steady pressure at 30.04 inHg, light southwest winds averaging 2.3 mph, and a low chance of light rain at 0.01 inches.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will likely hold in runs, seams, and near structure, feeding comfortably where light penetrates. Use a balanced nymph presentation with smaller natural patterns and stocked-attractor flies like Egg Pattern, Squirmy Worm, and Duracell for best results. Prioritize clean drifts and expect fish to stay close to cover under these conditions.
